channel.title: Teachings on Alan Aw
channel.description: Recent content in Teachings on Alan Aw

item.title: Stat 135 (Spring 2019)
item.description: I serve as a GSI for this upper division undergraduate course providing a first introduction to mathematical statistics. Based on problems provided by the Instructor Adam Lucas, I prepared and used slides to facilitate team work during section. I also occasionally wrote notes for students to supplement my in-class teaching. Here is a thematic summary I typed up for students to strategize for their final exam review. Week 1: Introduction Week 2: Sampling, parametric models / R practice Week 3: MoM estimation / bootstrap practice Week 4: Maximum likelihood estimation, CRLB [slides] Week 5: Sufficiency [slides] Week 6: Hypothesis testing, Neyman-Pearson lemma, duality of HT and CI [slides] [notes] Week 7: Comparing two samples: parametric Week 8: (Midterm review) [jeopardy slides] Week 9: Likelihood ratio test, UMP test, GLRT [slides] Week 10: (Spring break) Week 11: Comparing two samples: non-parametric, chi-square tests Week 12: ANOVA Week 13: Linear model (single predictor), least squares [notes] Week 14: Linear model (multiple predictors) Week 15: Bayesian statistics [slides]
